labels.evidence_hint: Look for creasing, lifted tabs, displaced shingles, torn seal strips, and directional patterns.
Look for impact marks, granule displacement, soft bruising, collateral metal marks, and matching elevations.
Document exposed underlayment, attachment pattern, adjacent lifting, and roof plane.
Document seal failure, fastener exposure, brittleness, and whether lift is mechanical or wind-related.
Photograph crease line, tab position, directionality, and surrounding field condition.
Separate uniform aging from impact-related displacement; include close-up and context photos.
Capture step flashing, counter flashing, apron flashing, exposed fasteners, rust, and sealant condition.
Document cracked neoprene, separation, fastener issues, and signs of leakage below.
Capture ridge orientation, cap cracks, missing caps, exposed nails, and ventilation condition.
Capture debris, abrasion, open seams, fastener placement, and water flow path.
Document intake, exhaust, blocked vents, attic evidence, and moisture/heat indicators.
Photograph plane deflection, attic framing if accessible, and moisture indicators.
Connect interior staining to exterior roof areas where possible; include room/location context.
Document moss, algae, lichen, shaded areas, drainage patterns, and surface deterioration.
Document fastening, nailing pattern, starter course, offset, flashing integration, and manufacturer deviations.
Photograph debris position, impact risk, nearby tree damage, and roof plane distribution.
Use context photos to document inspected areas and limitations.
Use only when no specific label applies; describe the condition clearly.
